title: Run first end-to-end game test (GatherWood in Minecraft)
description:
Validate that the full agent pipeline works against a real Minecraft server.

Pre-conditions:
1. Minecraft server running (vanilla or Paper, 1.16.4+ for pathfinder)
2. npm install run in MineflayerAdapter/ (includes mineflayer-pathfinder)
3. Agent:Enabled=true, Agent:Minecraft:* configured in appsettings.json

Test steps:
1. dotnet run --project WebUI.Blazor
2. Watch logs: should see 'Mineflayer adapter starting', 'Bot spawned', 'World adapter connected'
3. POST http://localhost:5000/api/agent/plan {goalName:GatherWood, parameters:{count:5}}
4. Watch bot navigate to a tree and mine 5 logs in-game
5. GET /api/agent/status -- verify Health > 0 and Goal = GatherWood

Success criteria:
- Bot spawns and C# receives spawn event; WorldState.Position updates
- MoveToTool dispatches move command (bot navigates to bot's current position = no-op for now)
- MineBlock command causes bot to auto-navigate to nearest oak_log within 64 blocks and dig it
- blockMined events increment WorldState.Inventory['oak_log']
- GatherWoodGoal.IsComplete returns true when oak_log count >= 5
- Loop stops; agent logs 'Goal GatherWood completed'

comments.author: AI Agent
comments.body:
First live test (2026-06-15): bot connected and spawned. Found 3 bugs:
1. No MoveTo in plan - bot never moved to trees
2. Overlapping mine commands caused 'Digging aborted' in Node.js
3. blockMined events not tracked - inventory stayed empty, IsComplete always false

comments.author: AI Agent
comments.body:
Fixes applied 2026-06-16:
- Bug0: MineBlockTool re-registered in Program.cs
- Bug1a: GatherWoodDecompose now includes MoveTo(bot.Position) before MineBlock
- Bug1b: Node.js mine case auto-navigates to nearest oak_log (64 block radius) before digging
- Bug2: Sequential command queue in index.js prevents overlapping dig commands
- Bug3: ProcessEventsAsync now parses blockMined/status/spawn/move events and updates WorldState.Inventory, Position, Food
- Bug3: WorldState.Builder gains AddInventoryItem and SetInventory helpers

Ready for second game test.
